<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Considering UI Structure Options**

I'm currently exploring the UI paradigms for the teacher management feature. The challenge lies in transitioning from the current drawer-based form to a master-detail view. I'm evaluating the integration of a wizard-like flow for creating new teachers, all while maintaining a smooth user experience.


**Mapping Architectural Components**

I'm now focusing on mapping the existing components to the desired "Master-Detail" architecture. I plan to refactor `TeacherList` to act as the central container. Then, I'll build a new `TeacherDetail` component to display the teacher information. The creation of an `AddTeacherWizard` component is next on the agenda.


**Re-architecting the User Interface**

I'm now diving into the specifics of refactoring. I've decided to rename `TeacherList.tsx` to `TeacherManager.tsx` and redesign its layout using a resizable panel to accommodate the sidebar and main content. The plan is to have a list of teachers in the sidebar and the `TeacherDetail` component, if a teacher is selected, or a default empty state in the main content area. Furthermore, I will create the `TeacherDetail` component, which will likely function with either an edit mode toggle or, perhaps more simply, an always-editable form, saving on the save button. The `AddTeacherWizard` component is next, as well. I will likely re-use form elements such as the `SubjectSelector` and `AvailabilityGrid` to maintain consistency across the system.


**Defining the Component Plan**

I'm now formulating a plan for the refactor. First, I'll build reusable components: `SubjectSelector`, `AvailabilityGrid`, and `TeacherConstraints`. Then, I'll assemble the `TeacherDetail` view and integrate it with the `TeacherSidebar` into a Master-Detail layout within `TeacherManagement`. Finally, I'll construct the `AddTeacherWizard`, reusing the components. I will start by looking for layout components in the existing code base.


**Designing the Component Structure**

I'm now establishing a component strategy. I plan to build reusable elements like `SubjectSelector` and `AvailabilityGrid`. After that, the `TeacherDetail` view, incorporating these, is next, followed by the sidebar. I will then integrate them into the `TeacherManager` layout, and lastly, create the `AddTeacherWizard`, reusing the components. I will also check for Drag and Drop dependencies.


</details></think>

```json
{
  "plan": "# Teachers Module Refactoring Plan\n\n## Goals\n- Transform the Teachers screen into a **Master-Detail** layout (Sidebar List + Main Detail View).\n- Implement a **Wizard-based** \"Add Teacher\" flow.\n- Improve UX with **Drag &amp; Drop** for subjects and **Visual Grid** for availability.\n- Ensure full **RTL** support and match the provided visual design.\n\n## Architecture &amp; Components\n\n### 1. Atomic Components (&#96;packages/web/src/features/teachers/components/ui/&#96;)\nWe will create reusable sub-components to be shared between the Detail view and the Wizard.\n- &#96;TeacherCard&#96;: Summary card for the sidebar list.\n- &#96;AvailabilityGrid&#96;: Interactive 7-day x Period grid (Green/Red toggle).\n- &#96;SubjectBucket&#96;: Drop zone for Primary vs. Allowed subjects.\n- &#96;ConstraintInput&#96;: Number inputs for max hours, etc.\n- &#96;TeacherAvatar&#96;: Reusable avatar component (using initials/color).\n\n### 2. Feature Components (&#96;packages/web/src/features/teachers/components/&#96;)\n- &#96;TeacherSidebar&#96;: Searchable list of teachers using &#96;TeacherCard&#96;.\n- &#96;TeacherDetail&#96;: The main view for editing a selected teacher.\n    - Uses a \"Save\" button header.\n    - Composed of \"cards\" (Basic Info, Subjects, Availability, etc.).\n- &#96;AddTeacherWizard&#96;: A 4-step modal/page (Basic -&gt; Subjects -&gt; Availability -&gt; Constraints).\n\n### 3. Layout (&#96;packages/web/src/features/teachers/&#96;)\n- &#96;TeachersPage&#96;: The main entry point, managing the layout state (selected teacher ID).\n    - **Layout**: &#96;div.flex.h-full&#96; -&gt; &#96;[Sidebar (w-1/4)]&#96; + &#96;[Main (w-3/4)]&#96;.\n\n## Implementation Steps\n\n### Step 1: Foundation &amp; UI Components\n- [ ] Create missing generic UI components: &#96;Card&#96;, &#96;Avatar&#96;, &#96;ScrollArea&#96; (in &#96;src/components/ui&#96;).\n- [ ] Create &#96;AvailabilityGrid&#96; component (interactive matrix).\n- [ ] Create &#96;SubjectBucket&#96; component using &#96;@dnd-kit&#96;.\n\n### Step 2: Teacher Sidebar &amp; List\n- [ ] Create &#96;TeacherCard&#96; component (Name, Subjects summary, Status).\n- [ ] Create &#96;TeacherSidebar&#96; with search input and virtualized/scrollable list.\n\n### Step 3: Teacher Detail View\n- [ ] Create &#96;TeacherDetail&#96; component.\n    - Header with \"Edit/Save\" actions.\n    - Section: Basic Info (Form fields).\n    - Section: Subjects (Two-column drag &amp; drop).\n    - Section: Availability (Grid).\n    - Section: Constraints &amp; Preferences.\n    - Section: Class Assignments Table.\n- [ ] Connect &#96;TeacherDetail&#96; to &#96;useUpdateTeacher&#96; hook.\n\n### Step 4: Add Teacher Wizard\n- [ ] Create &#96;AddTeacherWizard&#96; component.\n    - Implement Step Controller.\n    - Reuse UI components from Detail view.\n    - Final \"Review &amp; Save\" step.\n\n### Step 5: Integration\n- [ ] Update &#96;packages/web/src/features/teachers/index.ts&#96; to export new components.\n- [ ] Replace the existing &#96;TeacherList&#96; usage in &#96;routes&#96; (or wherever it's used) with the new &#96;TeachersPage&#96;.\n\n## Dependencies\n- &#96;@dnd-kit/core&#96;: Already installed.\n- &#96;lucide-react&#96;: Already installed.\n- &#96;shadcn/ui&#96;: We will manually add missing components based on standard implementation.\n",
```

```json
  "plan": "# Teachers Module Refactoring Plan (Dynamic)\n\n## Goals\n- Transform the Teachers screen into a **Master-Detail** layout (Sidebar List + Main Detail View).\n- Implement a **Wizard-based** \"Add Teacher\" flow.\n- ensure **NO HARDCODED VALUES**. All configuration (days, periods, subjects) must be dynamic.\n- Improve UX with **Drag &amp; Drop** for subjects and **Visual Grid** for availability.\n- Ensure full **RTL** support and match the provided visual design.\n\n## Architecture &amp; Components\n\n### 1. Atomic Components (&#96;packages/web/src/features/teachers/components/ui/&#96;)\nWe will create reusable sub-components to be shared between the Detail view and the Wizard.\n- &#96;TeacherCard&#96;: Summary card for the sidebar list.\n- &#96;AvailabilityGrid&#96;: **Dynamic** interactive grid.\n    - **Props**: &#96;days: string[]&#96;, &#96;periods: number&#96;, &#96;value: Record&lt;string, number[]&gt;&#96;, &#96;onChange&#96;.\n    - **Logic**: Renders rows based on &#96;days&#96; prop and columns based on &#96;periods&#96; prop.\n- &#96;SubjectBucket&#96;: Drop zone for Primary vs. Allowed subjects.\n    - **Props**: &#96;availableSubjects: Subject[]&#96;, &#96;primaryIds: string[]&#96;, &#96;allowedIds: string[]&#96;.\n- &#96;ConstraintInput&#96;: Number inputs.\n    - **Logic**: Set &#96;max&#96; attributes based on school config (e.g., max periods/day).\n- &#96;TeacherAvatar&#96;: Reusable avatar component.\n\n### 2. Feature Components (&#96;packages/web/src/features/teachers/&#96;)\n- &#96;TeacherSidebar&#96;: Searchable list of teachers using &#96;TeacherCard&#96;.\n- &#96;TeacherDetail&#96;: The main view for editing a selected teacher.\n- &#96;AddTeacherWizard&#96;: A 4-step modal/page.\n\n### 3. ---
```

The refactoring of the Teachers module is complete. I have implemented a Master-Detail layout with a dynamic Wizard for adding teachers, following your design requirements and ensuring all values are driven by the configuration.

### Changes Implemented

1.  **Atomic UI Components**:
    *   **`AvailabilityGrid`**: A dynamic grid component that renders based on `schoolConfig` (days and periods). It supports toggling availability (Red/Transparent) as requested.
    *   **`SubjectBucket`**: A Drag-and-Drop component for assigning subjects to "Primary" or "Allowed" lists using `@dnd-kit`.
    *   **`TeacherCard`**: A summary card for the sidebar showing avatar (initials) and primary subjects.
    *   **Generic Components**: Added `Card`, `Avatar`, and `ScrollArea` to `src/components/ui`.

2.  **Feature Components**:
    *   **`TeacherSidebar`**: A searchable list of teachers.
    *   **`TeacherDetail`**: The main edit view, composed of cards (Basic Info, Subjects, Availability, Constraints) inside a scrollable area.
    *   **`AddTeacherWizard`**: A 4-step modal wizard (Basic -> Subjects -> Availability -> Constraints) re-using the same components as the detail view.

3.  **Integration**:
    *   Created **`TeachersPage`** which orchestrates the Sidebar, Detail View, and Wizard.
    *   Updated **`Workspace.tsx`** to use `TeachersPage` instead of the old `TeacherList`.

### Key Features
*   **Dynamic Configuration**: The availability grid size and headers are fully dynamic based on your school configuration (Days of Week / Periods per Day).
*   **No Hardcoded Values**: All dropdowns and limits (e.g., max periods) respect the global config.
*   **RTL Support**: All components use logical properties and are ready for Persian/Dari layouts.

You can now navigate to the **Teachers** tab in your application to see the new Master-Detail layout. Click "Add New Teacher" to try the new Wizard flow.

**Pinpointing the Transparency**

I've just confirmed the CSS configuration is correct, translating `bg-background` to the intended cream color using HSL values. The real culprit seems to be the gradient in `dialog.tsx`. It's likely the combination of a solid background with a semi-transparent gradient, causing the perceived transparency issue, especially given the modal's height, as the base color under the gradient will show through.


**Analyzing the Rendering Order**

I'm now zeroing in on the rendering sequence. Since the screenshot clearly shows transparency, I'm verifying the layering. The `bg-gradient-to-b from-background to-muted /20` style is the culprit, especially given the modal's height. This gradient, combined with the underlying background, causes the transparency effect. By adding `bg-background` to the calling component, I force the solid color to render behind the gradient. I'm confident that this approach will resolve the transparency issue.


**Formulating the Implementation**

I'm now formulating the fix. The current plan is to add the `bg-background` class to the `DialogContent` component within `AddTeacherWizard.tsx`. This should render the solid background color *behind* the gradient, thus eliminating the transparency. Also, I will assess removing `overflow-hidden` from the `DialogContent` wrapper to see if it is unintentionally clipping the shadows of the Dialog. Furthermore, I intend to check the step indicator's visibility in the screenshot to ensure its lines are properly displayed.


**Refining the Solution**

```typescriptreact
    <Dialog open={open} onOpenChange={onOpenChange}>
      <DialogContent className="max-w-4xl h-[80vh] flex flex-col p-0 gap-0 overflow-hidden bg-background">
        {/* Header with Stepper */}
        <DialogHeader className="p-6 border-b bg-muted/10 relative overflow-hidden">
          <DialogTitle className="mb-6 text-xl text-center">
            {t('teachers.addTeacher', 'Add New Teacher')}
          </DialogTitle>
          
          <div className="relative flex items-center justify-between px-10">
            {/* Connector Line */}
            <div className="absolute left-10 right-10 top-4 h-[2px] bg-muted-foreground/20 -z-0" />
            
            {STEPS.map((s, i) => (
              <div key={s.id} className="flex flex-col items-center gap-2 relative z-10 bg-background px-2">
                <div
                  className={cn(
                    'w-8 h-8 rounded-full flex items-center justify-center text-xs font-bold transition-all border-2 shadow-sm',
                    step === s.id
                      ? 'bg-primary border-primary text-primary-foreground scale-110'
                      : step > s.id
                        ? 'bg-primary border-primary text-primary-foreground'
                        : 'bg-background border-muted text-muted-foreground'
                  )}
                >
                  {step > s.id ? <Check className="w-4 h-4" /> : s.id}
                </div>
                <span
                  className={cn(
                    'text-xs font-medium transition-colors',
                    step === s.id ? 'text-primary' : 'text-muted-foreground'
                  )}
                >
                  {t(`teachers.steps.${s.id}`, s.title)}
                </span>
              </div>
            ))}
          </div>
        </DialogHeader>

        {/* Content */}
        <div className="flex-1 overflow-y-auto p-6 bg-background">
```

---

I have addressed the issue with the "transparent" Add Teacher modal and improved the UI/UX as requested.

### Changes Made

1.  **Fixed Transparency**: Added `bg-background` to the main `DialogContent` in the Wizard. This ensures the modal is opaque and clearly visible against the overlay, fixing the issue where it appeared transparent.
2.  **Improved Stepper UI**:
    *   Added a **connector line** between steps to visualize progress clearly.
    *   Updated the completed steps to be **solid colored** (Primary color) instead of semi-transparent, for better visibility.
    *   Centered the step labels and improved spacing.
    *   Added a solid background to step indicators so the connector line doesn't show through them.

The "Add Teacher" wizard will now appear as a solid, well-defined modal with a clear progress indicator, matching the high-quality design of the rest of the application.

---
